Then only the people who have basically raised their hand and said that they're more interested by their actions, you only remark it to them afterwards. If you're trying to differentiate yourself in a marketplace where direct response is losing traction, the best way to do that is provide goodwill and then follow up with direct response after they've proven that they're interested. That covers the goodwill in the marketplace. This is ultimately like this is branding. If you're trying to brand yourself, this is how you do it. The key here is really providing value. There's no way to say that differently. The way that you have a good brand is by really providing good things. You want to be really clear about what you're trying to say and not just say things that are obvious that everyone would always guess. This will get you the most qualified leads and customers. And one thing that I have noticed across the board is that if you just have different color frames or even using pictures on the frames, it tends to give you more diversity in the actual ad. So you can actually get more runs with an ad. So I showed you guys a kale video earlier. We've used that video three times through each time taking it from zero to maximum hero. And then we changed the frame again, and we started zero again and take it all the way to hero. And so if you have a real winner, you don't want to just use it once. You want to recreate it, put different different colors on it, put different filters on it and use it again. And so you'll get way more run rate out of the ads when you do find winners, when you do that. All right. So that should conclude, yes, that should conclude the quality apps piece.
